description_long: This is an ancient-style ceramic vase with a tall, elongated ovoid body and a wide, flared mouth rim. The surface is adorned with horizontal bands of geometric and possibly zoomorphic motifs in faded earth tones, primarily dark brown, beige, and hints of reddish pigment. A single, robust handle connects the upper neck to the shoulder, curving gracefully and showing signs of wear. The rim is thick and slightly uneven, suggesting handcrafting and age, with some discoloration and surface abrasion. The body displays a matte, weathered finish, with visible patina and minor surface imperfections. This vase evokes a sense of antiquity and would be a striking decorative object in a public or private urban setting.
description: Tall, ovoid ceramic vase with a wide flared rim, single curved handle, and faded geometric motifs in earth tones. The surface is matte and weathered, showing signs of age and handcrafting.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full profile of the vase, with the handle visible on the right and the decorative bands clearly displayed.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the handle's attachment to the rim and shoulder, with more of the rim's thickness and wear visible.
description_view_2: Opposite angled view focusing on the handle's curve and the continuity of the decorative patterns around the body.
description_view_3: Rear view with the handle mostly obscured, emphasizing the back of the vase and the uninterrupted pattern bands.
